items.hook: Ezhimala, a hill reaching a height of 286 metres (938 ft), is located near Payyanur, in Kannur district of Kerala, South India.
items.body: It is a part of a conspicuous and isolated cluster of hills, forming a promontory, 38 km (24 mi) north of Kannur (Cannanore). The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Autonomous Republic of Northern Epirus was a short-lived, self-governing entity founded in the aftermath of the Balkan Wars on 28 February 1914, by the local Greek population in southern Albania.

items.hook: This type of land reclamation is also used along rivers.
items.body: A koog or groden is a type of polder found on the North Sea coast of Germany and Denmark that is established by the construction of dykes enclosing the land which is then drained to form marshland. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: After 11 years of construction, the Aswan High Dam in Egypt is completed.
items.body: The Aswan High Dam, often called simply Aswan Dam, is one of the world's largest embankment dams, which was built between 1960 and 1970 across the Nile in Aswan, Egypt. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Haemohormidium is a genus of parasitic alveolates in the phylum Apicomplexa.
items.body: They have two hosts in their life cycle: the vertebrate hosts are fish and the invertebrate vectors are leeches. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Eidophusikon was a piece of art, no longer extant, thought up by the English actor David Garrick and created by 18th-century French painter Philip James de Loutherbourg.
items.body: It opened in Leicester Square in London in February 1781. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Apollo program: At 02:56 UTC, astronaut Neil Armstrong becomes the first person to walk on the Moon, followed 19 minutes later by Edwin "Buzz" Aldrin.
items.body: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The world's lowest temperature in an inhabited location is recorded at Vostok Station, Antarctica at −89.2 °C (−128.6 °F).
items.body: The lowest natural temperature ever directly recorded at ground level on Earth is −89.2 °C at the then-Soviet Vostok Station in Antarctica on 21 July 1983 by ground measurements. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: It replaced the 1948 Ninth-of-May Constitution and was widely changed by the Constitutional Law of Federation in 1968.
items.body: The Constitution of the Czechoslovak Socialist Republic, promulgated on 11 July 1960 as the constitutional law 100/1960 Sb., was a communist state constitution and Czechoslovakia's third permanent constitution overall. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Venetia Anastasia, Lady Digby was a celebrated beauty of the Stuart period and the wife of a prominent courtier and scientist, Sir Kenelm Digby.
items.body: She was a granddaughter of Thomas Percy, 7th Earl of Northumberland and a great-granddaughter of Edward Stanley, 3rd Earl of Derby. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.body: A promontory is a raised mass of land that projects into a lowland or a body of water. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: It can also be defined as another hill or mountain range which projects in a lateral direction from a main hill or mountain range.
items.body: A spur is a lateral ridge or tongue of land descending from a hill, mountain or main crest of a ridge. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: American Civil War: First Battle of Bull Run: At Manassas Junction, Virginia, the first major battle of the war begins and ends in a victory for the Confederate army.
items.body: The American Civil War was a civil war in the United States between the Union and the Confederacy, which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Erden Eruç completes the first solo human-powered circumnavigation of the world.
items.body: Erden Eruç is a Turkish-American adventurer who became the first person in history to complete an entirely solo and entirely human-powered circumnavigation of the Earth on 21 July 2012 in Bodega Bay, California, United States. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Castration Celebration is a 2009 young adult novel by Jake Wizner.
items.body: The book was released on May 26, 2009, through Random House and follows the events of a group of friends during their summer together at Yale University’s summer arts program. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Inauguration of Leopold I of Belgium, first king of the Belgians.
items.body: Leopold I was the first king of the Belgians, reigning from 21 July 1831 until his death in 1865. One of those things you can't un-know.
